title: pteridium caudatum subsp. arachnoideum
intro: Pteridium caudatum subsp. arachnoideum, also known as the New Zealand bracken, is a subspecies of the Pteridium caudatum fern, native to New Zealand. It has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant contains compounds that have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against cell damage. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and salves to support overall health and well-being.
